advantages: Captures any statistical dependence, including non-linear and non-monotonic relationships that Pearson correlation can miss.
Symmetric and non-negative with a clear zero point (independence); interpretable in the same bit units as Shannon entropy.
Model-free filter for feature selection—no training loop required to score I(X_i; Y) from data.
Connects cleanly to KL divergence and entropy decomposition, unifying information-theoretic tools in ML pipelines.
disadvantages: Harder to estimate reliably from finite samples than correlation, especially for continuous variables—binning and k-NN estimators introduce bias and variance.
Does not reveal direction of influence; high I(X; Y) does not mean X causes Y (same as correlation).
Raw MI scales with support size; comparing MI across datasets with different cardinalities requires normalization (e.g., NMI).
Computation cost grows with feature count and sample size; exhaustive MI ranking on high-dimensional data needs approximations or subsampling.
